name: Modified Citrus Pectin
historicalUse: Citrus fruits and their preparations have been used medicinally across Arabic, Jewish, and Mediterranean traditions for over 2,000 years. Rambam specifically recommended citrus preparations in his Treatise on Poisons and other medical writings for their detoxifying and protective properties. Ibn Sina documented lemon and citrus preparations extensively in the Canon. Modified citrus pectin as a fractionated, low-molecular-weight form is a modern pharmaceutical development, but the citrus substrate has deep classical roots.
classicalSources: Ibn Sina — Canon of Medicine (citrus therapeutics)
modernEvidence: PectaSol-C® is a clinically validated, pH- and temperature-modified low-molecular-weight citrus pectin with the ability to pass through the intestinal wall into systemic circulation — unlike standard pectin. Primary mechanism is galectin-3 inhibition. Galectin-3 is a protein implicated in cancer metastasis, fibrosis, and chronic inflammation. Clinical research by Dr. Isaac Eliaz (developer of PectaSol-C®) demonstrates significant galectin-3 reduction and clinically meaningful results in prostate cancer monitoring (PSA studies) and heavy metal chelation.
mechanisms: Galectin-3 inhibition — blocks galectin-3, a key mediator of cancer cell adhesion, metastasis, and fibrosis
Heavy metal chelation — low-MW pectin binds lead, arsenic, and cadmium in the GI tract and systemically
Immune modulation — activates NK cell and macrophage activity via TLR-4 binding
Anti-fibrotic — galectin-3 inhibition reduces tissue fibrosis in heart, liver, and kidney
benefits: Supports cellular resilience and healthy galectin-3 balance
Supports detoxification of environmental heavy metals
Supports anti-fibrotic pathways in aging tissues
safetyProfile: Excellent safety profile. PectaSol-C® is well tolerated. Mild GI effects (bloating, loose stools) at high doses. GRAS status for citrus pectin. Fractionated form has extensive clinical safety data.
contraindications: Caution in patients with severe GI motility disorders
drugInteractions: May reduce absorption of some medications if taken simultaneously — take medications 2 hours apart from MCP
Chemotherapy — galectin-3 inhibition may have synergistic effects with some agents; discuss with oncologist
physicianNotes: PectaSol-C® is the only clinically validated MCP form — other modified citrus pectins lack this evidence base. Particularly relevant in integrative oncology (galectin-3 monitoring, PSA surveillance protocols) and in heavy metal detoxification programs. Advise oncologists of use in cancer patients — generally considered supportive rather than interfering.
patientGuidance: Take on an empty stomach or at least 2 hours away from other medications. If you are being monitored for cancer markers or are in an integrative oncology program, this ingredient is particularly relevant — discuss with your physician.
